label: Send a notification
description: Send a clear, trustworthy message that informs the user about something that has happened or prompts them to take a next step, without requiring them to stay in the service.
purpose: Keep users informed about service events and prompt action without requiring active session presence.
use_when: To confirm an action or outcome (e.g. booking confirmed, application submitted).
To remind users about a time-bound event (appointments, deadlines, expiry).
To notify users of a status change or decision.
To prompt users to return to the service to continue a task.
To send authentication or security messages (e.g. OTPs).
do_not_use_when: The message itself must be a legal or official document.
The service requires the user to reply with information — use ask-a-question instead.
The message would include sensitive personal data.

inputs.label: Recipient reference
inputs.description: User identifier or contact reference for routing the notification.

inputs.label: Message content
inputs.description: Structured message content including subject, body, reference number, and next steps.

ux_considerations.text: Do not rely on a single channel. Always provide an alternative if the primary channel fails.
Messages must clearly identify the sender (official service name) to prevent fraud.
Respect user contact preferences. Provide opt-in and opt-out choices.
Keep messages short and readable. Avoid images as the only way to convey meaning.
Avoid exposing sensitive information on shared or insecure devices.

technical_notes.text: Notification delivery must be logged with timestamp, channel, status, and recipient reference.
Failed notifications must trigger a retry or fallback channel attempt.
